title: Week 1 Environmental Infrastructure Observation Brief
summary: Field observation of environmental and infrastructure conditions at Igbo-Etche Central Market and the Eneka–Igbo Etche Link Road corridor revealed severe drainage gaps, waste accumulation, and flood exposure during the rainy season.
shortSummary: Drainage deficit, solid waste accumulation, and unfinished road drainage are exposing the community to flood and erosion risk.
location: Igbo-Etche, Etche LGA, Rivers State, Nigeria

sitesVisited.name: Igbo-Etche Central Market
sitesVisited.type: Local market square / community trading centre

sitesVisited.name: Eneka–Igbo Etche Link Road
sitesVisited.type: Access road under construction / transit corridor

geography.geomorphology: Niger Delta coastal plain — low-lying alluvial terrain
geography.climate: Tropical humid / equatorial — active rainy season June–October
geography.riskNote: Flood-prone classification due to extremely low mean sea level altitude.

keyFindings: The market square and surrounding stall access paths have no formal drainage, causing pooled water after rainfall.
Solid waste is routinely disposed of on open ground or near the community stream, increasing siltation and public health risks.
The active road corridor remains without completed side drains in key stretches, increasing erosion and waterlogging exposure.
Streamside drainage paths are blocked by sediment and debris, threatening food production and water quality.

images.caption: Igbo-Etche Central Market: waste accumulated by the roadside; no collection point present.
Igbo-Etche Streamside / Roadside: flooded roadside and waterlogged access path to farmland.

immediateActions: Clear accumulated solid waste from the market square and streamside area immediately.
Enforce a halt on open waste burning within the market zone.
shortTermActions: Complete side drains along the Eneka–Igbo Etche Link Road within the active rainy season window.
Establish a designated waste collection point for the central market.
mediumTermActions: Design and install market drainage infrastructure to manage runoff.
Launch community sensitisation on waste, flooding, and water quality.
ongoingActions: Repeat field assessments after rainfall to monitor changes.
Build a community environmental stewardship group.
Continue GPS-georeferenced monitoring for advocacy and partner reporting.
